definition: Extended monetary transmission lag episodes resolve via sudden discontinuity rather than gradual adjustment in 3 out of 4 historical cases (1966-69, 2004-07, 2018-19). Only 1994-95 (preemptive tightening) was an exception. This suggests the modal resolution for the current extended lag is a discontinuous catch-up event rather than a slow grind.
evidence: Identified as most important single insight in debates
open_questions: Does the sample of only four episodes provide sufficient statistical power?
What novel features of the current cycle could change the pattern?
What would be the channel for discontinuous catch-up in the current cycle?
